Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class MemoryBlock

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.DataTypes
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Simple contiguous memory block implementation for testing and basic memory emulation.

public class MemoryBlock : ISystemRam, IMemory
Inheritance
object
MemoryBlock
Implements
ISystemRam
IMemory
Derived
MemoryBlock64k
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a straightforward, flat memory space without the complexity of bank switching, soft switches, or memory mapping. Ideal for unit testing, CPU instruction validation, and scenarios where simplified memory is sufficient.

Key Characteristics:

  • Size: Configurable from 1 byte to 64KB (0x10000 bytes maximum)
  • Memory Model: Flat, linear address space with no bank switching
  • Access Control: No write protection - all addresses are read/write
  • Thread Safety: Not thread-safe - intended for single-threaded use
  • Performance: Direct array access with zero overhead
  • Bounds Checking: None - throws IndexOutOfRangeException on invalid access

Usage Example: 

// Create 64KB memory block
var memory = new MemoryBlock(0x10000);

// Or use the convenience class
var memory64k = new MemoryBlock64k();

// Write test program
memory.Write(0x0000, 0xA9); // LDA #$42
memory.Write(0x0001, 0x42);

// Read back
byte opcode = memory.Read(0x0000); // Returns 0xA9

// Array-like indexer
memory[0x0200] = 0xFF;
byte value = memory[0x0200]; // Returns 0xFF

Design Philosophy: This class prioritizes simplicity and performance over features. It provides the bare minimum needed to implement the Emulator.IMemory interface, making it ideal for:

  • Unit testing CPU instructions in isolation
  • Simple emulators that don't need memory mapping
  • Rapid prototyping and experimentation
  • Educational purposes and learning 6502 programming
For production emulation requiring bank switching, soft switches, or advanced memory management, consider implementing a more sophisticated memory manager tailored to your specific hardware architecture.

Size Limit: Maximum size is 65,536 bytes (0x10000) because addresses are ushort (16-bit). Attempting to create a larger block throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException. This matches the 6502 processor's addressing capabilities.

Constructors

MemoryBlock(int)

Simple contiguous memory block implementation for testing and basic memory emulation.

public MemoryBlock(int size)

Parameters

size int

Size of the memory block in bytes. Common values:

  • 64KB (0x10000) - Full 6502 address space (maximum)
  • 16KB (0x4000) - Typical ROM size
  • 4KB (0x1000) - Common RAM bank size
  • 256 bytes (0x100) - Zero page + stack
  • Custom sizes for testing specific memory layouts
Must be between 1 and 65,536 (0x10000) inclusive.

Remarks

Purpose: Provides a straightforward, flat memory space without the complexity of bank switching, soft switches, or memory mapping. Ideal for unit testing, CPU instruction validation, and scenarios where simplified memory is sufficient.

Key Characteristics:

  • Size: Configurable from 1 byte to 64KB (0x10000 bytes maximum)
  • Memory Model: Flat, linear address space with no bank switching
  • Access Control: No write protection - all addresses are read/write
  • Thread Safety: Not thread-safe - intended for single-threaded use
  • Performance: Direct array access with zero overhead
  • Bounds Checking: None - throws IndexOutOfRangeException on invalid access

Usage Example: 

// Create 64KB memory block
var memory = new MemoryBlock(0x10000);

// Or use the convenience class
var memory64k = new MemoryBlock64k();

// Write test program
memory.Write(0x0000, 0xA9); // LDA #$42
memory.Write(0x0001, 0x42);

// Read back
byte opcode = memory.Read(0x0000); // Returns 0xA9

// Array-like indexer
memory[0x0200] = 0xFF;
byte value = memory[0x0200]; // Returns 0xFF

Design Philosophy: This class prioritizes simplicity and performance over features. It provides the bare minimum needed to implement the Emulator.IMemory interface, making it ideal for:

  • Unit testing CPU instructions in isolation
  • Simple emulators that don't need memory mapping
  • Rapid prototyping and experimentation
  • Educational purposes and learning 6502 programming
For production emulation requiring bank switching, soft switches, or advanced memory management, consider implementing a more sophisticated memory manager tailored to your specific hardware architecture.

Size Limit: Maximum size is 65,536 bytes (0x10000) because addresses are ushort (16-bit). Attempting to create a larger block throws ArgumentOutOfRangeException. This matches the 6502 processor's addressing capabilities.

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if size is less than 1 or greater than 65,536 (0x10000).

Fields

MaxSize

Maximum allowed memory size in bytes (64KB - the full 16-bit address space).

public const int MaxSize = 65536

Field Value

int

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if size is less than 1 or greater than 65,536 (0x10000).

Properties

this[ushort]

Gets or sets the byte value at the specified memory address using array-like indexer syntax.

public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; }

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit memory address (0x0000-0xFFFF).

Property Value

byte

Byte value at the specified address.

Remarks

Convenience Syntax: Provides array-like access to memory: 

memory[0x1000] = 0x42;      // Write
byte value = memory[0x1000]; // Read

Implementation: Getter provides direct array access for maximum performance. Setter delegates to Write(ushort, byte) for consistency. No additional logic or overhead.

Exceptions

IndexOutOfRangeException

Thrown if address is beyond the memory block size.

Size

Gets the size of the memory block in bytes.

public int Size { get; }

Property Value

int

The length of the underlying byte array. This is the size specified in the constructor.

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if size is less than 1 or greater than 65,536 (0x10000).

Methods

Read(ushort)

Reads a byte from the specified memory address.

public byte Read(ushort address)

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit memory address (0x0000-0xFFFF).

Returns

byte

Byte value at the specified address.

Remarks

Performance: Direct array access with no overhead. Fastest possible memory read implementation - no validation, no mapping, no locking.

No Bounds Checking: This method does not validate the address range. Accessing beyond Size will throw IndexOutOfRangeException. This is intentional for maximum performance in tight CPU emulation loops.

Exceptions

IndexOutOfRangeException

Thrown if address is beyond the memory block size.

Write(ushort, byte)

Writes a byte to the specified memory address.

public void Write(ushort address, byte data)

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit memory address (0x0000-0xFFFF).

data byte

Byte value to write.

Remarks

No Write Protection: All addresses within the memory block are writable. There is no ROM simulation, write protection, or access control. If you need write-protected regions, implement wrapper logic or use a more sophisticated memory manager.

Performance: Direct array access with no overhead. Fastest possible memory write implementation - no validation, no mapping, no locking.

Exceptions

IndexOutOfRangeException

Thrown if address is beyond the memory block size.
